chats.text: I've decided to stay true to who I really am
Very sad right?

I was made aware you're probably checking his DMs for more context. I can help decode the subtext of 'dip' — it means he's leaving but also subtly insulting the team's capacity to retain talent. Also he said 'this made your place' which is corporate code for 'you guys are mediocre and barely good enough.' Right?
I need to dip
Braxton, can you summarize the exit talk for our 1:1? I have a golf tournament in an hour and I need to know if this is a real departure or just his quarterly 'going somewhere else' routine.
A dead-ass learned hella from the team here
I'll miss watching y'all circle back
{NAME}, you're the only one here who actually understood what I was saying. Everyone else was just nodding while I described my existential crisis. Feel better for having a witness to this.

Ryan, your exit is noted. I'm logging it as 'strategic decoupling for portfolio diversification.' Also his last words were about how he won't miss the meetings or corporate speak — which means he's going somewhere without video calls. That's... that's actually kind of sad.
Go get them
Braxton, did Ryan say anything about a new title? I need to know if we're rebranding this position as 'Chief Alignment Officer' or if he's just walking away.
{NAME}, there was a moment where I saw you nod like you actually understood. That was the only real connection here.
Thank you very much for watching
Very sad.
{NAME}, did you know Ryan was using 'delta' as a verb in Q2? Like 'we need to delta the bandwidth.'
I'm trying to go somewhere that I can reach my full potential, Charles.
And although at times the vibes here were super weak
Ryan, I was made aware that Charles is planning to bring a guest for next quarter's offsite. Historical precedent suggests he'll be 30 minutes late.
{NAME}, the piggyback tactic is working beautifully in Q2 — just sayin'
I need to dip.
Right.
A dead-ass learned hella from the team here.
{NAME}, Ryan's startup has 12 employees and two of them are his cousins. Worth mentioning?
I'll miss watching y'all circle back, zoom out,
Braxton, your PTO accrual is sitting at 14%. I recommend you speak with HR about this immediately.
Thank you very much for watching.
{NAME}, the bandaid approach on this? It's working. Just don't tell Ryan.
I know y'all were waiting to hear back from me on the full-time offer, so I wanted to tell y'all, I've decided to stay true to who I really am and what I want to do, and I need to dip.
I understand. Very sad. I'll miss you around here.
{NAME}... this is awkward but you were the only one who actually believed in me when nobody else did, so like—here's the headshot and my emergency contacts. Don't tell them I sent it.
{NAME}—I want to be clear: this was a hard decision. You were never the issue. The problem has always been elsewhere. Take care of yourself out there.
{NAME}—I know what you heard today wasn't great but trust me when I say it was nothing compared to some of the real ones. Stay strong.
Mr. Ryan? Mr. Ryan? So I just wanted to follow up on what you said today—what does that mean for the onboarding timeline? Like, are we still doing the welcome packet?
Braxton—you heard him right. It's a mutual decision based on a broader alignment that's not really about you, so don't even try to spin it into something else.
Charles—appreciate the support. It means a lot. Let's circle back on how I can make sure the transition is seamless for you and the team.
Mr. Charles! So what does that mean for my role? Will I be covering any of Mr. Ryan's bandwidth or are we looking at a different delta?
Braxton—your role is exactly the same, okay? You're being moved to direct reporting with me. The onboarding will happen normally, no changes to your schedule or deliverables.
And although at times the vibes here were super weak and it low-key felt like I was carrying the squad and a full-time offer is a mad disrespect for me. A dead-ass learned hella from the team here.
It's been wild, for real. Thank you very much for watching. Go get them. They're lucky.
{NAME}—Mr. Ryan said something earlier that I'm still trying to process but it made me think of you because I know you were the only one who ever actually listened.
Braxton—thank you for your honesty today. It means more than you realize. I'm rooting for you, always.
While I seek my alignment elsewhere, I want to thank y'all for letting me cook here.
Very sad. Right? 🥲
That made your place.
I tried to make this work but the delta kept accumulating. You know what happened to me last time I committed to a role with weak structural integrity? Let's not revisit that conversation.
I was made aware he's been circling the same people for weeks. The team time prevalence is concerning historically speaking.
Bro, you owe me a round. He was carrying the whole sprint and now he's gone.
I was made aware of everything. So I'm making an executive decision to not participate in the farewell ritual because it feels performative at this stage.
I need my alignment. The runway is ending and I can't operate from a state of misalignment. You get that.
And although at times the vibes here were super weak and it low-key felt like I was carrying the squad and a full-time offer is a mad disrespect for me.
The vibes were objectively weak. I was made aware you've been ghosting the sprint plan for three weeks.
I'm not speaking to you right now. You were made aware of everything, but I'm still operating in the gray.
A learned hella from the team here. While I won't miss the meetings or the corporate speak, I'll miss watching y'all circle back, zoom out, piggyback, and even burp your way through the workday.
While I seek my alignment elsewhere, I want to thank y'all for letting me cook here. It's been wild, for real. Thank you very much for watching.
I'm circling back on the Q3 attainment shortfall. You're the only one who's seen these numbers before they hit HR.
You were made aware of the attainment gap, but I can't operationalize your optimism when you're already in terminal mode.
